---
output:
  xaringan::moon_reader:
    css: ["default", "extra.css"]
    lib_dir: libs
    seal: false
    nature:
      highlightStyle: github
      highlightLines: true
      countIncrementalSlides: false
      ratio: '16:9'
---

```{r, echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE}
##xaringan::inf_mr()
## For offline work: https://bookdown.org/yihui/rmarkdown/some-tips.html#working-offline
## Images not appearing? Put images folder inside the libs folder as that is the main data directory

library(tidyverse)
library(readxl)
library(stargazer)
##library(kableExtra)
##library(modelr)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE,
                      eval = TRUE,
                      error = FALSE,
                      message = FALSE,
                      warning = FALSE,
                      comment = NA)
```

class: slideblue

.size80[**Today's Agenda**]

<br>

.size45[
1. Review APA citation format

2. What makes evidence high quality?

3. Brainstorm answers to our paper prompt
]

<br>

.center[.size40[
  Justin Leinaweaver (Spring 2022)
]]

???

## Prep for class
1. Prep Google Doc for collecting citations (prompt and names)

2. Send email with link for the class

https://docs.google.com/document/d/1abEx0dYki6yiFvJ1FE7SCBwNiBWzi0tOk8DEfUEN-Q8/edit?usp=sharing





---

class: middle, center, slideblue

.size60[**Who or what is currently the biggest international threat to the United States?**]

.size40[
Bring one piece of .textred[**HIGH QUALITY**] published evidence you can use to answer the question.

Be ready to provide the class with a correctly formatted, .textred[**APA style citation**] for your piece of evidence (see the Purdue OWL for guidance).
]

???

We had an assignment for today.

Everybody submit your citation to the shared document.

Link in your email.

<br>

HAS EVERYONE SUBMITTED THEIR CITATION TO THE SHARED GOOGLE DOC?



---

class: middle, slidegreen

.size50[**APA Style (Source: Purdue OWL)**]

### Books
.size30[Author, A. A. (Year of publication). *Title of work: Capital letter also for subtitle*. Publisher Name. DOI]

### Journals, Magazines, Newspapers
.size30[Author, A. A., Author, B. B., & Author, C. C. (Year). Title of article. *Title of Periodical*, volume number(issue number), pages. DOI]

### Webpage / Online Content
.size30[Lastname, F. M. (Year, Month Date). *Title of page*. Site name. URL]

???

The Purdue OWL is a super handy reference for formatting questions.

Please get comfortable doing this by hand BEFORE you start relying on automated software to do it for you.

If you do use a web service, YOU MUST review your citations to make sure they are correct!

Web services are handy but they make LOTS of mistakes.

<br>

Ok, take a few minutes and review all of the sources.

ARE ALL OF THESE FORMATTED CORRECTLY?

Remember, the goal here is to help each other.
- You're not calling anybody out!
- We want everyone to format correctly!

<br>

**Incorrect citations must be corrected by that student.**

ANY QUESTIONS ON APA FORMATTING OF CITATIONS?


---

class: middle, center, slidegreen

.size50[**What is .textred[**HIGH QUALITY**] evidence?**]

<br>
<br>

```{r, fig.align='center', out.width='100%'}
knitr::include_graphics("libs/Images/03_1-engle2018.png")
```

???

Now let's talk high quality evidence.

WHAT DOES IT MEAN TO SAY A SOURCE OR PIECE OF EVIDENCE IS HIGH QUALITY?

(- Focus on the "initial appraisal" section from the "Critically Analyzing Information Sources" reading.)

(High quality evidence:)
- Author: Credentials, Affiliation, Frequently cited by experts
- Date: Up-to-date is preferred
- Edition or revision: Multiple editions imply demand; Revisions imply being kept up-to-date
- Publisher (if book): University press
- Title of journal: Scholarly journal

"Scholarly or peer-reviewed journal articles are written by scholars or professionals who are experts in their fields. In the sciences and social sciences, they often publish research results."

<br>

Keep in mind, the quality of the evidence at least partially depends on what you are using it to do.

Establishing basic facts? Newspaper is probably sufficient.

Using someone else's argument? Better be built on peer-reviewed research.

<br>

Ok, let's now take a few minutes to review all of the submitted citations to evaluate the quality of the sources.

Again, the goal here is to ensure everyone writes a convincing argument in their first paper and that requires high quality evidence!

ARE ALL OF THESE EXAMPLES OF EVIDENCE THAT IS HIGH ENOUGH QUALITY? WHY OR WHY NOT?

<br>

ANY QUESTIONS ON TYPES OF EVIDENCE AND WHAT WE MEAN BY HIGH QUALITY?



---

class: middle, center, slideblue

.size60[**Who or what is currently the biggest international threat to the United States?**]

<br>
<br>

.size50[Discuss the options in our cases.]

???

Let's hear our options!

Go around the room and give us your proposed answer to the question.



---

class: middle, slidepurple

.size70[**For Wednesday**]

<br>

.size50[
1. Read Farrell (2010),

2. Pick a single answer for your paper, 

3. Brainstorm 8-10 reasons you picked this answer.

]

???

I know this sounds like a lot of reasons, but I want you to push yourself to build a big lost of possible premises.

You're going to narrow down to three or four in the end, but we want to intentionally start very broadly.

QUESTIONS ON THE ASSIGNMENT?
